violations.text: The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by a cambro. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2025

Salsa, cheese and shredded lettuce were observed held at 46-49 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were placed into refrigeration and/ or replaced. PIC informed to cease placement of cold toppings over steam table. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2025

The floors in the walk-in cooler area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Patched repair has been made since last inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2026

The grout between tiles in dishwashing area was observed to missing or worn down. Tile observed missing near soda syrups causing water accumulation. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/17/2024
The walk-in cooler gaskets were observed in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026
Openings observed by takeout counter and wall near back door. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 01/16/2026
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by a cambro. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025

-Raw animal foods such as raw shelled eggs were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods such as limes in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. -Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination (raw chicken over raw beef). Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Sanitizer bottle was replaced and primed during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025

Shredded lettuce and salsa was observed held at 44-52 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were placed into refrigeration and additional ice was added to cold topping bar. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings in storage area and drink station. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 12/09/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the shelving under soda machine have an accumulation of old droppings. Area was cleaned and sanitized during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025
The floors in the walk-in cooler area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2026

The grout between tiles in dishwashing area was observed to missing or worn down. Tile observed missing near soda syrups causing water accumulation. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/17/2024

The following has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris: -interior of kitchen hoods (especially around ansul lines) -under and around cookline equipment The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2025
The coving by ice machine was observed in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026
The walk-in cooler gaskets were observed in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/02/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. FMC expired, once food safety certificate is obtained (class and test scheduled for next week), PIC to certify individual with Rhode Island certified food protection manager license. Proof of registration to be sent to inspector. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024
The drainage pipe for the bar hand sink was observed to be disconnected from the bottom of hand sink and draining onto floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

The dumpster and storage areas were observed with an accumulation of old shelving, old equipment and other debris. Refuse area must be kept clean and maintained to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

The floor in the dishwashing area is in poor repair. The grout between tiles was observed to missing or worn down. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/17/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. FMC expired, once food safety certificate is obtained, PIC to certify individual with Rhode Island certified food protection manager license. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Establishment must sanitize all utensils and dishes at 3 bay sink. PIC placed a service call during time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2024

Various taco toppings and sides were observed held at 48-60 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were placed over additional ice during time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2024

The presence of insects is not controlled as evidenced by live flies at time of inspection. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2024

Foil was observed being used as lining for prep table shelving and was observed to be flaking off of table and with an accumulation of food and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces should be smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the kitchen dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 06/17/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the can opener holster, and robot-coupe base, and shelving in the walk-in cooler have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024
The drainage pipe for the bar hand sink was observed to be disconnected from the bottom of hand sink and draining onto floor. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

The dumpster and storage areas were observed with an accumulation of old shelving, old equipment and other debris. Refuse area must be kept clean and maintained to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024
The wall in the bar area underneath fireball dispenser was observed to have a hole. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

The floors behind equipment in back room by rear door as well as dish area and bar have an accumulation of soil residue, droppings and/or food debris. The Ansul pipe and sides of ventilation hood were observed to have grease droplets forming on equipment. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024

The floor in the dishwashing area is in poor repair. The grout between tiles was observed to missing or worn down. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/17/2024

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in the dishwashing area. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2024
